Which of the following reactions could be taking place inside the flask? (Flask at $25^{\circ}C$ and $32^{\circ}C$ shown)
Which of these properties is most closely associated with the polarity of a molecule?

The empty graduated cylinder has a mass of 172.6 grams. Each screw has a density of 5.91 g/mL and a volume of 4.90 mL. Five screws are added to the empty graduated cylinder, and then a liquid with a density of 1.23 g/mL is added to the cylinder. How many mL of the liquid must be added to bring the total mass of the entire system (cylinder, screws, and liquid) to 335.0 g?

Ordinary table sugar can be used as a rocket fuel in model rockets when it is oxidized using potassium nitrate. The unbalanced equation for the reaction is $KNO_{3}(s) + C_{12}H_{22}O_{11}(s) \rightarrow N_{2}(g) + CO_{2}(g) + H_{2}O(g) + K_{2}CO_{3}(s)$. How many moles of gas are generated from the oxidation of 30 grams of sugar using this reaction?

Four students who never agree on anything are asked to make a water sample at exactly 50.0 $^{\circ}C$. One student runs 500. mL of water from the faucet and checks the temperature. It is 23.3 $^{\circ}C$. "That's too cold," says the second student, who then adds 525 mL of 90.5 $^{\circ}C$ water to the mix. "Now it's too hot," says the third student, who dumps in 25 grams of ice at -5 $^{\circ}C$. The fourth student shrugs his shoulders and adds another 165 mL of water from the faucet. What is the final temperature of the water? (Assume the density of water is 1 g/mL.)
If a fission reaction of a U-235 nucleus produces I-131 and three neutrons, which other isotope will be formed?
Five circuits similar to the one shown here were made, each one using a different aqueous solution in the beaker. Which of the solutions below would result in the light bulb glowing the brightest?

0.100 moles of propane gas ($C_{3}H_{8}$), 0.100 moles of methane gas ($CH_{4}$), and 0.900 moles of $O_{2}$ gas are placed in a flexible-walled container at 25.0 $^{\circ}C$ and 1.00 atm pressure and a complete combustion reaction occurs. After the reaction is complete the container is heated to 190.0 $^{\circ}C$. What will the final volume of the gas inside the container be?
How many milligrams of magnesium would remain dissolved in solution if 500. L of 0.10 M $Mg(NO_{3})_{2}$ is mixed with 225 L of 0.45 M NaOH? The $K_{sp}$ for $Mg(OH)_{2}$ is $5.6 \times 10^{-12}$.
Which of these compounds would you expect to have the largest a constant in the van der Waals equation: $(P + a \frac{n^{2}}{V^{2}})(V - nb) = nRT$?

The standard enthalpy of combustion for pentane ($C_{5}H_{12}$) is -3509 kJ/mol. What is the change in internal energy of the system for the combustion of one mole of liquid pentane at 25 $^{\circ}C$ and 1 atm pressure? (Ignore the volume of any liquids in the reaction.)

The gas phase reaction $2A + B \rightleftharpoons 3C + D$ has a $\Delta H$ of 25.5 kJ and an equilibrium constant K of $2.40 \times 10^{-3}$ at 25 $^{\circ}C$. What is the equilibrium constant for this reaction at 200 $^{\circ}C$?

If you add 25.0 grams of $Ca(OH)_{2}$ to 9.00 L of water and then dilute it to a final volume of 10.0 L using 0.50 M HCl and let it reach equilibrium, how many grams of solid $Ca(OH)_{2}$ will remain undissolved in the container?

You intended to dilute 30.0 mL of 0.015 M solution of hydrochloric acid to twice the original volume, then add 0.015 grams of NaOH, but instead of diluting the solution with deionized water you accidentally diluted it with 0.0050 M sodium acetate instead. What will the pH of the final solution be after you add the NaOH?

Dolomite is a calcium magnesium carbonate mineral that occurs widely in pre-Cenozoic sedimentary rocks, but is much less common in Cenozoic sedimentary rock beds. One mechanism for its formation involves magnesium ions replacing calcium ions in calcite: $2CaCO_{3} + Mg^{2+} \rightarrow CaMg(CO_{3})_{2} + Ca^{2+}$. What is the oxidation state of the carbon atom in dolomite, $CaMg(CO_{3})_{2}$?

A 1.5 L bulb and a 2.3 L bulb are connected by a valve. At 25 $^{\circ}C$ the larger bulb contains $Cl_{2}$ gas at 2.8 atm and the smaller bulb contains $Cl_{2}$ gas at an unknown pressure. When the valve is opened and the entire system is heated to 265 $^{\circ}C$, the final pressure in the combined system is 5.2 atm. What was the initial pressure of $Cl_{2}$ in the smaller bulb at 25 $^{\circ}C$ before the valve was opened? Assume the volume of the valve tube is negligible.

The exothermic reaction $A + B + C \rightarrow D + E$ exhibits first order kinetics with respect to each reactant and is third order overall. Under which of these reaction conditions would the reaction exhibit first-order kinetics?

Phase changes A, B, C, D, E, and F are the six usual phase changes that any matter such as $H_{2}O$ undergoes. Use the clues below to determine which phase change corresponds to vaporization. I. Phase change E is exothermic. II. Phase changes E and C are equal in magnitude and have the lowest magnitude of the phase changes. III. Phase change A is greater in magnitude than phase change F and both are exothermic. IV. Phase change B is equal in magnitude to phase change F.

You have just finished up a titration of HCl with NaOH and reached a beautiful faint pink phenolphthalein endpoint when a vile prankster creeps up behind you, throws some NaOH pellets into your titration flask, and runs away. You add 25.00 mL of 1.50 M HCl to the flask to turn the bright pink solution clear again, and then you continue the titration. If it takes an additional 17.88 mL of 1.20 M NaOH to reach the endpoint, and if each NaOH pellet has a mass of 0.1605 grams, how many pellets did the prankster throw into your flask?

In basic solution metallic zinc reacts with the permanganate ion to produce solid manganese dioxide and insoluble zinc hydroxide. If you add a 96.0 g piece of zinc to large beaker containing 2.50 L of a 0.177 M solution of potassium permanganate and the reaction goes to completion, how many grams of solid will there be in the beaker when the reaction is finished?

You are making propanol molecules ($C_{3}H_{8}O$) out of elemental carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen. You have 1.75 moles of $H_{2}$, $6.45 \times 10^{23}$ carbon atoms, and 15.4 grams of $O_{2}$. You make as many propanol molecules as you can, and then you decide to combust the propanol you made using the remaining $O_{2}$. How many $CO_{2}$ molecules result from the combustion?
